TextENGNew: The day after that.
changeReason: JPN 'あの日の翌日' means 'the day after that [day]'; old translation 'The next day' is generic, new 'The day after that' correctly specifies referencing a particular previous day.

TextENGNew: ...It was a strange day. Mion was desperately trying to act normal.
changeReason: New translation changes 'regain her composure' to 'act normal', shifting from internal state to external behavior; JPN '平静を取り戻そうとやっきになっている' supports both but 'act normal' better matches the performative aspect.

TextENGNew: Every time our eyes happened to meet, she'd claim she'd just remembered something she had to do, or that she needed the bathroom, and slip away.
Rena, unable to stand watching from the sidelines, had appointed herself Mion's full-time caretaker.
"...Mion-san is acting completely bizarre today."
"I know everything that happened, but... it's difficult to explain..."
"...There are times when girls become emotionally unstable. The wisest course is simply to leave her be."
Glancing up, I saw Rena beckoning me from the hallway. ...What is it?
changeReason: Old has narrator wondering in past tense 'I wondered what it was'; new uses present tense 'What is it?' reflecting inner monologue, and 'turning around' vs 'glancing up' are different physical actions.

TextENGNew: "Keiichi-kun, over here, over here."
"What is it, calling me out here like this? Some kind of secret?"
"Well... um... it sounds like you had quite the ordeal."
changeReason: Old 'It looks like it was a disaster' vs new 'you had quite the ordeal'; JPN '災難だったみたいだね' - old implies observation of a disaster, new correctly conveys sympathy for a personal ordeal directed at the listener.

TextENGNew: "...Ahahahaha."
"Uh... yeah... I guess so..."
changeReason: Old 'Uh... Yea~h' sounds like agreement; new 'Uh... yeah... I guess so...' adds uncertainty/reluctance that better matches the JPN hesitation markers.

TextENGNew: "Mii-chan was pretty much in pieces, but I've managed to put her back together somehow."
"Impressive that you managed that... How did you, exactly...?"
"The same way I fix the television. Just come at it from a forty-five-degree angle and—whack! Whack!"
changeReason: Sound effects changed from 'HIYAAAA!' (martial arts yell) to 'whack! Whack!' (impact sounds); JPN 'えい！ えい！' is more of an action exclamation, closer to 'whack' than a battle cry.

TextENGNew: Saying this, she mimicked a karate chop.
...I genuinely cannot tell if she's joking or not.
changeReason: Old uses second person 'You know I can't tell' (addressing reader/someone); new uses third person 'she's joking' reflecting inner monologue. JPN uses no addressee, so new is more accurate.

TextENGNew: "She'll be fine from tomorrow. But there's something I need from you too, Keiichi-kun."
"...I know I didn't mean any harm, but for some reason I still feel guilty. ...I'll hear whatever it is."
changeReason: Old 'I'm listening' vs new 'I'll hear whatever it is'; JPN '何でも聞くぞ' means 'I'll listen to anything' - old is more passive/present, new better conveys willingness to comply with any request.

TextENGNew: "Good. Thank you. ...You see, Mii-chan is going to pretend the last few days simply never happened."
Pretend it never happened, huh... ...I see. There's an escape route through that door, at least.
changeReason: Old 'well, that's one way to run from it' vs new 'There's an escape route through that door, at least'; new uses a metaphor not present in JPN, while old is closer to the literal meaning of 'そういう逃げ方もある'.

TextENGNew: "So you see— Keiichi-kun, I need you to do the same. Pretend it never happened, and Mii-chan will go back to her normal self."
"J-Just doing something that simple... will it really be okay...?"
"Please—just give her some space until Mii-chan gets her feelings sorted out."
"...All right... I understand."
I shouldn't have been at fault in any of this... and yet for some reason I feel like I'm the guilty one.
The real culprit here is Shion, who spent an entire day impersonating Mion while knowing full well what was going on...!!!
Ugh... but... she never once actually said she was Mion... ...Ugh. Where the hell am I supposed to direct this fist?!
changeReason: Old 'What am I supposed to be angry at!?' vs new 'Where the hell am I supposed to direct this fist?!'; JPN 'このやり場のないゲンコツ' literally means 'this fist with nowhere to go' - new correctly translates the fist imagery absent in old.

TextENGNew: Rena watched me fuming and stomping the ground in frustration, and let out a quiet laugh.
"But Keiichi-kun, now you know too, don't you. That Mii-chan has her cute side."
changeReason: Old 'you knew as well' (implies prior knowledge) vs new 'now you know too, don't you' (implies new realization); JPN 'わかったでしょ' means 'you understand now, right?' - new is more accurate.

TextENGNew: Rena spoke in a composed voice, her hair drifting in the breeze.
"...Yeah. I've learned that she's someone who'll never let you get bored."
"...Good."
Rena gave that brief reply, then stretched languidly and tilted her face up toward the sky.
"As long as you see that, Keiichi-kun, these past few days will soon become nothing more than a good story to laugh about."
Following her lead, I stretched and looked up at the sky as well.
The sky was clear and impossibly high.
"No club activities or anything today. I'm sorry, Keiichi-kun, but just for today—could you walk home alone? It'll all be back to normal by tomorrow, just today. ...Okay?"
If leaving her alone for one day was all it took to get things back to normal, that was an easy enough request.
changeReason: Old 'it was an easy decision to make' vs new 'that was an easy enough request'; JPN '楽な注文だ' literally means 'an easy order/request' - new correctly translates '注文' as request rather than decision.

TextENGNew: "Got it. ...I can't say this to Mion directly, so I'll say it through you. ...I feel kind of bad about all this. I want to apologize."
"...Hmm! Well, when you get down to it, the root of the problem was that Keiichi-kun said something thoughtless. Let this be a lesson—try to develop a little tact."
changeReason: Old interjection 'Ah, no!' vs new 'Hmm!'; JPN 'う～ん！' is a thinking sound, not a negation. New correctly renders it as a contemplative sound.

TextENGNew: "Yes ma'am—I'll make an effort."
That day turned out to be the shortest day of my life.
Rena stayed by Mion's side the entire time, talking the hours away.
It looked like such a lively conversation that I was almost tempted to join in—but I had made a promise.
Mion, too, seemed to be giving her utmost effort to remain unaware of my existence.
...So I returned the favor, doing my utmost to remain unaware of hers.
I ate lunch alone for the first time in a while.
Mion ate with just Rena.
changeReason: Old 'Mion and Rena ate theirs by themselves' implies both were alone/isolated; new 'Mion ate with just Rena' correctly conveys they ate together as a pair. JPN '魅音もレナと二人きりで食べた' means Mion ate with just Rena.

TextENGNew: I was heaving gray sighs when Rika-chan wandered over and patted me on the head.
"...Both Keiichi and Mii-chan did a lot of studying today, didn't they."
changeReason: Old 'learned a whole lot' vs new 'did a lot of studying today'; JPN 'いっぱいお勉強しましたですね' uses 'benkyou' (studying). New adds 'today' and preserves the studying metaphor that Keiichi reacts to in the next line.

TextENGNew: Somehow "studying" was the last word I expected her to use. ...It made the whole thing sound like some kind of romantic complication. I didn't like that at all.
changeReason: Old 'I'd never say it like that' vs new 'studying was the last word I expected her to use'; old misses the point - Keiichi is surprised Rika used the word 'studying', not that he wouldn't say it that way.

TextENGNew: I just hoped the teacher wouldn't notice something was off and call us to the staff room after school...
"...I'm sure Keiichi will grow up to be a wonderful person. ...pat-pat."
"...Oh? Well, Rika-chan is the studious type, so you're on track to become quite a wonderful adult yourself, aren't you?"
"...Of course. When I grow up, I'm going to be spectacularly, spectacularly amazing."
changeReason: Old 'super amazing' vs new 'spectacularly, spectacularly amazing'; JPN 'スゴイスゴイ' repeats the word twice, which new preserves with the double 'spectacularly' while old uses only a single intensifier.

TextENGNew: "And when I grow up, I'm going to be an absolutely magnificent lady, you know~?"
"Satoko, I'd wager good money you'll still be exactly as childish as you are now, no matter how old you get."
"Wha-wha-wha-WHAAAAAT did you just say!?!?"
Satoko snapped her fingers, and a washtub came plummeting from above, striking me squarely on the head.
"Owwwww...! What do you think you're doing, Satokoooo!!!!"
*crash* *bang*! ...Pinching and scratching...!!
changeReason: Old '*pinch* *squeeze*' vs new 'Pinching and scratching'; JPN 'つねったりひっかいたり' means 'pinching and scratching' - old incorrectly has 'squeeze' where it should be 'scratch'.

TextENGNew: It was a day that made me truly appreciate the value of having friends.
Walking home from school alone—this might actually be my first time since I moved here.
Rena and Mion headed home together ahead of me, while Satoko and Rika-chan were called to the staff room by the teacher.
Now that I was alone, the sudden stillness pressed in around me, and loneliness welled up from somewhere deep inside.
...What am I getting sentimental about? It's only one day. Just one day.
By tomorrow, Mion will surely be her usual energetic self again.
I had walked all the way to the school gate when an unexpected silhouette stopped me in my tracks.
"Kei-chan..."
That voice—Shion...?! No, wait, this is...
"Can we please stop that already..."
"A... ahaha... You figured it out already?"
The person standing there with downcast, bashful eyes may have looked exactly like Shion—but the person inside was Mion!
Face flushed scarlet, Shion—no, Mion—looked up at me from beneath lowered eyes.
"Um... I thought maybe I could be more honest this way."
"I'm sorry..."
"There's nothing to apologize for, is there?"
"...I'm sorry."
"...About yesterday. Aha. ...I thought... if I was dressed as Shion, I could apologize to you properly."
"You have nothing to apologize for. If anything, I was the one at fault. ...But—please stop dressing up as Shion."
"If you said the same thing as Mion, I wouldn't laugh at you for it."
Mion's face grew redder still.
She faltered, flustered, groping for a reason why she couldn't simply be herself.
In that moment, I thought Mion looked incredibly endearing.
"...That was adorable."
The moment I'd said it, I didn't know where to look.
This is my first time saying something like that to anyone!!
changeReason: Old 'it's not like I've ever said stuff like this' (double negative, somewhat dismissive) vs new 'This is my first time saying something like that to anyone' (direct, emphatic). New more clearly conveys it's a genuinely new experience.

TextENGNew: What's happening here?! Am I actually attempting to court Mion right now?! What's come over me?! Get a grip, Keiichi!!
changeReason: Old 'Am I trying to seduce Mion' vs new 'Am I actually attempting to court Mion'; 'seduce' has sexual/manipulative connotations while 'court' implies romantic pursuit. JPN '口説く' can mean both but 'court' better fits the innocent context.

TextENGNew: ...After a thorough internal panic, I stole a glance at Mion.
Mion, for her part, had turned completely crimson and was just standing there, steam practically rising from her head.
Strangely, watching her like that helped calm me down.
"Because she's Mion, she acts like one of the guys. Because she's Shion, she acts like a girl. But that's not right at all. You're sisters. You're both girls..."
changeReason: Old 'Mion is manly. Shion is feminine.' states it as fact; new 'Because she's Mion, she acts like one of the guys. Because she's Shion, she acts like a girl.' frames it as role-based behavior. JPN uses 'だから' (because/therefore), supporting the new causal framing.

TextENGNew: "...Kei-chan."
"From now on, we've also got Mion: Girls' Edition. I'm going to tease both versions equally—they're both 'Mion,' after all!"
changeReason: Old 'From now on we also have Mion: Female Edition. I'll tease the both of equally, since you're both Mion!' vs new version. JPN says he'll tease both 'versions' of Mion as 'Mion'; old says 'you're both Mion' addressing twins, new correctly addresses two sides of the same person.

TextENGNew: "Kei-chan... ...Y-Yeah!"
Mion nodded with the most radiant smile I'd ever seen from her.
changeReason: Old 'incredible smile' vs new 'the most radiant smile I'd ever seen from her'; new adds specificity ('I'd ever seen from her') and 'radiant' conveys a different quality than 'incredible'. JPN 'とびきりの笑顔' means an exceptionally bright/special smile.

TextENGNew: "And I'm going to openly tease you right back, Kei-chan—for every time you got all flustered around my girly side!"
"What?! When was I ever flustered?!"
Although several instances came to mind immediately...
"Hehehe... I think you'd better search your own conscience on that one."
W-well, that might be... true.
Mion teased me for getting all worked up, and dashed off with that mischievous grin. I ran after her.
Her steps were light. Somehow I was glad—glad that she'd regained her energy and found her way back to herself...
changeReason: Old 'I'm happy that I was able to get her back to normal and realize how great she actually is' implies Keiichi made her realize her own greatness; new 'glad that she'd regained her energy and found her way back to herself' is about Mion finding herself. JPN focuses on Mion discovering her own qualities.
